$comment: D-CSUBSET-INDEX-INTEGER-PROMOTION: a `char` subscript of a wide-element (int) array/pointer integer-promotes to int (C 6.3.1.1) before the byte-scaling Mul. Overflow-sensitive: a char index 100 of an int array (stride 4) needs offset 400 — at char width that wraps (400 mod 256) and reads the wrong element. Exercises array-base + pointer-base + a char-indexed STORE + signed-char. Exit 42 iff promotion is correct. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the cst_to_hir Index promotion arm -> char index stays Char -> scaleIndexToBytes walls at the sub-native ALU gap (compile fail) or overflows (a[36] not a[100]) -> exit 7. The release arm re-witnesses under the shipped pipeline (runtime char-var index, loop-filled array — nothing folds).
